title: rubus keleterios
intro: Rubus keleterios, also known as the New Zealand thimbleberry, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, providing various health benefits such as improving cardiovascular health, boosting the immune system, and acting as an anti-aging agent. Rubus keleterios can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
